revision.text:
[[ja:阿倍王子神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Abe Ōji Shrine | image = File:Abeoji-jinja1.jpg | location = [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], [[Abeno Ward]], [[Abeno Motomachi]] 9-4 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.631030555555554|N|135.50913888888888|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Ōji deity|ja|lt_ja=熊野王子神|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} ({{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=イザナギ|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=イザナギ}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=イザナミ|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q682306}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|速素盞嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}})
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} | Established = Reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} (393–427 CE) | Annual Festival = | Map = {{location map|Japan Osaka city|label=Abe Ōji Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=|qq=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Abeno-ku|qid=Q489009|1=Abeno-ku, Osaka}} | deity = {{ill|Kumano Gongen|qid=Q11568925|1=Kumano Gongen}} }} '''Abe Ōji Shrine''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== '''Abe Ōji Shrine''' (阿倍王子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Abeno-ku, Osaka|en|Abeno-ku, Osaka|qid=Q489009|lt=Abeno-ku, Osaka|1=Abeno-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|qid=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}}. Formerly known simply as '''Abe Ōji''' (阿倍王子), it is one of the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|qid=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kodo's Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kodo's Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kodo's Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}—subsidiary shrines dedicated to {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}. == History == [[File:Bird Omikuji.jpg|alt=Okimono omikuji from Abeno Oji Shrine|thumb|[[Okimono omikuji]] from the shrine]] According to the ''Setsushū Tōsei-gun Abe Gongen Engi'' (摂州東成郡阿倍権現縁記), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} (393–427), the emperor dreamed of a {{ill|Yatagarasu|en|Yatagarasu|qid=Q745463|lt=Yatagarasu|1=Yatagarasu}} (three-legged crow) sent by Kumano Sanzan. When he ordered a search, a similar crow was found in the area (once an island called Abejima), leading to the founding of the shrine at its current site. Another theory suggests that the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}}, an influential ancient family based in the region after the capital moved to {{ill|Naniwa Nagara-Toyosaki Palace|en|Naniwa Nagara-Toyosaki Palace|qid=Q11659570|lt=Naniwa Nagara-Toyosaki Palace|1=Naniwa Nagara-Toyosaki Palace}}, established the shrine alongside their family temple, ''Abe-dera'' (阿倍寺). The shrine survived various historical shifts, including its revival in 826 CE by {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|qid=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}}. Later, it became a significant {{ill|Ōji shrine|ja|王子社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135500654|1=Ōji shrine}} along the {{ill|Kumano Kaidō|ja|熊野街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kaidō|1=Kumano Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11568976}}, with {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|en|Emperor Go-Toba|qid=Q316658|lt=Emperor Go-Toba|1=Emperor Go-Toba}} visiting during his Kumano pilgrimages in the late Heian period. Today, Abe Ōji Shrine is the only Ninety-Nine Ōji shrine in Osaka Prefecture that remains at its original location. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Main Deities''': {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Ōji-kami|ja|熊野王子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—{{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}}, and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} == Precincts == * '''Main Hall (Honden)''' – Rebuilt in 1967 * '''Worship Hall (Haiden)''' – Rebuilt in 1967 * '''Former Main Hall''' (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) – Built in 1908 * '''Kuzunoha Inari Shrine''' (葛の葉稲荷神社) – Associated with the mother of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|qid=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei|1=Abe no Seimei}} * '''Okarasu Shrine''' (御烏社) – Dedicated to Yatagarasu * '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''', '''Water Shrine''', and various sacred trees == Abe no Seimei Shrine == Located ~50m north of Abe Ōji Shrine, dedicated to the legendary onmyōji, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|qid=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei|1=Abe no Seimei}}. * '''Main Hall (Registered Tangible Cultural Property)''' – Built in 1925 * '''Worship Hall (Registered Tangible Cultural Property)''' – Built in 1925 * Statues of Abe no Seimei and Kuzunoha (a fox spirit) == Cultural Properties == * '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)''' ** Former Main Hall ** Kuzunoha Inari Shrine Main Hall ** Abe no Seimei Shrine Main Hall, Worship Hall, and Fencing == Access == * Osaka City Bus (Routes 62, 63, 64, 67) – Get off at Ōji-chō Stop * {{ill|Hankai Uemachi Line|en|Hankai Uemachi Line|qid=Q5648519|lt=Hankai Tramway Uemachi Line|1=Hankai Uemachi Line}} – 3 min walk from Higashi-Tengachaya Station * {{ill|Midōsuji Line|en|Midōsuji Line|qid=Q1192413|lt=Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|1=Midōsuji Line}} – 12 min walk from Shōwachō Station == Related Pages == * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|qid=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} * {{ill|Seimei Shrine|en|Seimei Shrine|qid=Q276830|lt=Seimei Shrine|1=Seimei Shrine}} * {{ill|Kuzunoha Inari Shrine|ja|信太森葛葉稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuzunoha Inari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuzunoha Inari Shrine|1=Shinodanomori Kuzunoha Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11386029}} * {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Ueno Ōji (Osaka)|ja|上野王子 (大阪市)|lt=Ueno Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Ōji (Osaka)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Ōji (Osaka)|1=Ueno Ōji Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11360400}} – '''Abe Ōji Shrine''' – {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori Ōji|1=Tsumori Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11555995}} {{Shinto Sidebar|qq=}} {{shinto-stub|qq=}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} {{draft categories| [[ja:阿倍王子神社]] }} [[ja:安倍野王子]] [[zh:安倍野王子]] {{translated page|ja|阿倍王子神社|version=104198004|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Abeoji-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11657249}} [[Category:Japanese buildings completed in 1908]] [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:安倍氏]] [[Category:晴明丘・北畠]] [[Category:Seimei Shrines]] [[Category:阿倍野区の建築物]] [[Category:阿倍野区の歴史]]
